sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, you want sweetness to be kept in check.
sections.subtitleText: 0.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, you want sweetness to be kept in check.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 9.3g of carbs. sugar, glycemic index, and fibre are doing most of the work. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.
sections.subtitleText: 9.3g of carbs. sugar, glycemic index, and fibre are doing most of the work. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 1.1g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. for a fruit, being sweet is normal, but controlling it is what matters.
sections.subtitleText: 1.1g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. for a fruit, being sweet is normal, but controlling it is what matters.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 59% daily value stands out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin c at 59% daily value stands out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first: hydrating whole-fruit package because water and whole-food structure make it easier to use well than juice or sweets. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

displayItems.explanation: Water and whole-food structure make it easier to use well than juice or sweets.

sections.subtitleText: pros first: hydrating whole-fruit package because water and whole-food structure make it easier to use well than juice or sweets. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: rarely eaten as a stand-alone fruit because that weakens the real-world staple argument. sugar can climb fast because a lot of fruits look healthy but still lean sweet. satiety is not especially strong because fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal.

closing.summary: Lemon has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
